structured_payload_candidate.title: Zone Air Distribution Effectiveness Ez Determination (ASHRAE 62.1)
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Determine Ez based on DAT relative to zone space temperature. If DAT <= zone space temperature, Ez = EzC (default 1.0); if DAT > zone space temperature, Ez = EzH (default 0.8).
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote:
Determine zone air distribution effectiveness Ez.
1. If the DAT at the terminal unit is less than or equal to zone space temperature, Ez shall be equal to EzC (default to 1.0 if no value is scheduled).
2. If the DAT at the terminal unit is greater than zone space temperature, Ez shall be equal to EzH (default to 0.8 if no value is scheduled).

anchor_repair_reason: evidence_quote replaced with verbatim section heading line

structured_payload_candidate.title: Zone Outdoor Airflow Voz Calculation and Override Priorities (ASHRAE 62.1)
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Voz = (Vbz-A* + Vbz-P*)/Ez. Modifications in priority order: 1) If zone not in Occupied Mode, or window open, Vbz-P*=0, Vbz-A*=0, Vmin*=0. 2) If occupancy sensor unpopulated and occupied-standby allowed: same zeros. 3) Else if occupancy sensor unpopulated but no standby: Vbz-P*=0, Vmin*=Vmin. 4) If CO2 sensor, apply DCV logic.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Override ventilation components and possibly Vmin* per priority.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote:
f. The required zone outdoor airflow Voz shall be calculated as Voz = (Vbz -A* + Vbz -P*)/Ez, where the normal values of Vbz -A* and Vbz -P* are modified if any of the following conditions are met, in order from higher to lower priority:
1. If the zone is in any mode other than Occupied Mode , and for zones that have window switches and the window is open: Vbz -P* = 0, Vbz -A* = 0, and Vmin* = 0.
2. If the zone has an occupancy sensor, is unpopulated, and occ upied -standby mode is permitted: Vbz -P* = 0, Vbz -A* = 0, and Vmin* = 0 .
3. Else, if the zone has an occupancy sensor, is unpopulated, but occupied -standby mode is not permitted: Vbz -P* = 0 and Vmin* = Vmin.

evidence_quote:
f. The required zone outdoor airflow Voz shall be calculated as Voz = (Vbz -A* + Vbz -P*)/Ez, where the normal values of Vbz -A* and Vbz -P* are modified if any of the following conditions are met, in order from higher to lower priority:
1. If the zone is in any mode other than Occupied Mode , and for zones that have window switches and the window is open: Vbz -P* = 0, Vbz -A* = 0, and Vmin* = 0.
2. If the zone has an occupancy sensor, is unpopulated, and occ upied -standby mode is permitted: Vbz -P* = 0, Vbz -A* = 0, and Vmin* = 0 .
3. Else, if the zone has an occupancy sensor, is unpopulated, but occupied -standby mode is not permitted: Vbz -P* = 0 and Vmin* = Vmin.

f. The required zone outdoor airflow Voz shall be calculated as Voz = (Vbz -A* + Vbz -P*)/Ez, where the normal values of Vbz -A* and Vbz -P* are modified if any of the following conditions are met, in order from higher to lower priority:
1. If the zone is in any mode other than Occupied Mode , and for zones that have window switches and the window is open: Vbz -P* = 0, Vbz -A* = 0, and Vmin* = 0.
2. If the zone has an occupancy sensor, is unpopulated, and occ upied -standby mode is permitted: Vbz -P* = 0, Vbz -A* = 0, and Vmin* = 0 .
3. Else, if the zone has an occupancy sensor, is unpopulated, but occupied -standby mode is not permitted: Vbz -P* = 0 and Vmin* = Vmin.

structured_payload_candidate.title: CO2 Demand Controlled Ventilation Loop (P-only) for Zones
structured_payload_candidate.summary: During Occupied Mode, a P-only loop maintains CO2 concentration at setpoint; reset from 0% at setpoint minus 200 PPM to 100% at setpoint. Loop is disabled and output set to zero when zone is not in Occupied Mode.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: P-only control loop with specified reset range and disable outside Occupied.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote:
ii. During Occupied Mode , a P-only loop shall maintain CO 2 concentration at setpoint ; reset from 0% at setpoint  minus 200 PPM and to 100% at setpoint .
iii. Loop is disabled and outp ut set to zero when the zone is not in Occupied Mode .

evidence_quote:
ii. During Occupied Mode , a P-only loop shall maintain CO 2 concentration at setpoint ; reset from 0% at setpoint  minus 200 PPM and to 100% at setpoint .
iii. Loop is disabled and outp ut set to zero when the zone is not in Occupied Mode .

structured_payload_candidate.title: CO2 DCV Reset of Vmin* and Vbz-P* for Cooling-Only, Reheat, Series Fan-Powered, and Dual-Duct VAV Terminal Units
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: VAV terminal units (cooling-only, reheat, series fan-powered, dual-duct with mixing control)
structured_payload_candidate.summary: The CO2 control loop output resets Vmin* from Vmin at 0% loop output up to Vcool-max at 100% loop output, and resets Vbz-P* from 0 cfm (0 L/s) at 0% up to Vbz-P at 100% loop output, in parallel.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Apply parallel reset of Vmin* and Vbz-P* based on CO2 loop output.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: (a) The CO2 control loop output shall reset both the occupied minimum airflow setpoint  (Vmin*) and the population component of the required breathing  zone outdoor airflow (Vbz -P*) in parallel. Vmin* shall be reset from the zone minimum airflow setpoint  Vmin at 0% loop output up to maximum cooling airflow setpoint Vcool -max at 100% loop output. Vbz -P* shall be reset from 0 L/s (0 cfm) at 0% loop output up to the Vbz -P at 100% loop output.

evidence_quote: (a) The CO2 control loop output shall reset both the occupied minimum airflow setpoint  (Vmin*) and the population component of the required breathing  zone outdoor airflow (Vbz -P*) in parallel. Vmin* shall be reset from the zone minimum airflow setpoint  Vmin at 0% loop output up to maximum cooling airflow setpoint Vcool -max at 100% loop output. Vbz -P* shall be reset from 0 L/s (0 cfm) at 0% loop output up to the Vbz -P at 100% loop output.
evidence_text: (a) The CO2 control loop output shall reset both the occupied minimum airflow setpoint  (Vmin*) and the population component of the required breathing  zone outdoor airflow (Vbz -P*) in parallel. Vmin* shall be reset from the zone minimum airflow setpoint  Vmin at 0% loop output up to maximum cooling airflow setpoint Vcool -max at 100% loop output. Vbz -P* shall be reset from 0 L/s (0 cfm) at 0% loop output up to the Vbz -P at 100% loop output.

structured_payload_candidate.title: CO2 DCV Reset of Vbz-P* for SZVAV AHUs (ASHRAE 62.1)
structured_payload_candidate.summary: The minimum outdoor air setpoint MinOAsp is equal to Voz. The CO2 control loop output resets the population component Vbz-P* from 0 cfm at 0% loop output up to Vbz-P at 100% loop output.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Reset Vbz-P* per described range; MinOAsp set to Voz.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: (a) The minimum outdoor air setpoint  MinOAsp is equal to Voz. The CO2 control loop output shall reset the  population component of the required breathing zone outdoor airflow Vbz -P* from 0 L/s (0 cfm) at 0% loop output up to Vbz -P at 100% loop output.

evidence_quote: (a) The minimum outdoor air setpoint  MinOAsp is equal to Voz. The CO2 control loop output shall reset the  population component of the required breathing zone outdoor airflow Vbz -P* from 0 L/s (0 cfm) at 0% loop output up to Vbz -P at 100% loop output.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Zone-Des-OA-min Determination (Title 24)
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Zone-Des-OA-min is equal to, in priority: i) zero if window switch and window open; ii) zero if occupancy sensor unpopulated and permitted occupied-standby; iii) larger of Varea-min and Vocc-min otherwise.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote:
2. Zone -Des-OA-min is equal to the following , in order from highest to lowest priority :
i. Zero if the zone has a window switch and the window is open .
ii. Zero if the zone has an occupanc y sensor , is unpopulated , and is permitted to be in occupied -standby mode per Section 3.1.1.2.b.3 .
iii. The larger of Varea -min and Vocc -min otherwise .

evidence_quote:

i. Zero if the zone has a window switch and the window is open .
ii. Zero if the zone has an occupanc y sensor , is unpopulated , and is permitted to be in occupied -standby mode per Section 3.1.1.2.b.3 .
iii. The larger of Varea -min and Vocc -min otherwise .

structured_payload_candidate.title: Vmin Calculation (Title 24)
structured_payload_candidate.summary: If Vmin in Section 3.1.2 is “AUTO”, Vmin shall equal Zone-Abs-OA-min; else Vmin as entered.

structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Reset MinOAsp linearly between MinOA and DesOA.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Time-Averaged Ventilation Mode Entry and Pulse Width Modulation
structured_payload_candidate.summary: When Vspt is nonzero and less than Vm, enter TAV mode. TAVratio = Vspt/Vm. TCT = 15 min (adj). Open period OP = max(1.5 min, TCT * TAVratio). Closed period CP = TCT - OP. During closed period, VAV damper disabled with output 0. At end of closed period, damper commanded to last open position before reenabling loop. Cycles alternate until Vspt > Vm. On first entry, start with open period RNDM*OP, where RNDM is random 0.0-1.0.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote:
5.2.2.1.  When the active airflow setpoint  Vspt is nonzero  and is less than the lowest possible airflow setpoint  allowed by the controls (Vm), the airflow setpoint  shall be pulse width modulated as follows:
a. The time-averaged ventilation ( TAV ) ratio shall be determined as TAVratio = Vspt/Vm
b. The total cycle time (TCT) shall be 15 minutes (adjustable)
c. Open period . During the open period, the TAV airflow setpoint  Vspt * shall be equal to Vm for a period of time OP, which is the larger of  the following :
d. 1.5 minutes or
e. TCT multiplied by TAVratio
f. Closed period.  During the closed period, Vspt* shall be set to 0 for a period of time CP, where CP = TCT – OP. The VAV damper con trol loop shall be disabled with output set equal to 0 during the closed period. At the end of each closed period, the VAV damper shall be commanded to the last position from the previous open period prior to reenabling  the control loop.
g. During TAV mode, e ach cycle shall consist of an open and closed period that alternate until Vspt is greater than Vm.
...
h. When first entering TAV mode, start with an initial open period of duration RNDM*OP, where RNDM is a random number between 0.0 and 1.0 .

evidence_quote: 5.2.2.1.  When the active airflow setpoint  Vspt is nonzero  and is less than the lowest possible airflow

structured_payload_candidate.title: CO2 Sensor Low Reading or Unoccupied Elevated CO2 Alarm
structured_payload_candidate.summary: If CO2 concentration is less than 300 ppm, or the zone is in Unoccupied Mode for more than 2 hours and zone CO2 concentration exceeds 600 ppm, generate a Level 3 alarm indicating sensor may be out of calibration.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: CO2 < 300 ppm OR (Unoccupied > 2 hr AND CO2 > 600 ppm).
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote:
5.2.2.3.  For zones with CO 2 sensors:
a. If the CO 2 concentration is less than 300 ppm, or the zone is in Unoccupied Mode  for more than 2 hours and zone CO 2 concentration exceeds 600 ppm, generate a Level 3 alarm. The alarm text shall identify the sensor and indicate that it may be out of calibration.

structured_payload_candidate.title: CO2 Concentration Exceeds Setpoint Alarm
structured_payload_candidate.summary: If the CO2 concentration exceeds setpoint plus 10% for more than 10 minutes, generate a Level 3 alarm.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Selection Between ASHRAE 62.1 and Title 24 Ventilation Logic
structured_payload_candidate.summary: The engineer must select between ventilation logic options: if complying with ASHRAE Standard 62.1, use Section 5.2.1.3 and delete Section 5.2.1.4; if complying with California Title 24, use Section 5.2.1.4 and delete Section 5.2.1.3.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Follow one of the two paths and delete the other.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote:
The engineer must select between ventilation logic options:
If the project is to comply with ASHRAE Standard 62.1  ventilation requirements, use Section 5.2.1.3  and delete Section 5.2.1.4 .
If the project is to comply with California Title 24 ventilation requirements, use Section 5.2.1.4  and delete Section 5.2.1.3 .

evidence_quote:

If the project is to comply with California Title 24 ventilation requirements, use Section 5.2.1.4  and delete Section 5.2.1.3 .

structured_payload_candidate.title: Occupied-Standby Mode Classification
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Occupied-standby mode applies to individual zones, is considered a zonal subset of Occupied Mode, and is not considered a zone-group operating mode.
